entry.title: Quantum Damping of Cosmological Shear: A New Prediction from Loop Quantum Cosmologies
entry.summary: We study the dynamics of the Bianchi I universe in modified loop quantum cosmology (mLQC-I) and uncover a robust mechanism for isotropization: the shear is dynamically suppressed after the bounce and decays rapidly in the quantum post-bounce regime, independently of the equation of state of standard matter sources. This naturally drives the Universe toward a homogeneous and isotropic expanding phase without fine-tuning. Our results show that mLQC-I provides a new quantum-gravitational mechanism for suppressing anisotropies, absent in other bounce models.
entry.arxiv:comment: revtex4-2, 2 figures and no table, v2: extended clarifications added to the manuscript, v3: version to be published in PRL, v4:typos corrected, version published in PRL
